title: DATA-DRIVEN JOINT CHANCE-CONSTRAINED OPTIMIZATION FOR THE WORKOVER RIG SCHEDULING PROBLEM
Resource-Constrained Project Scheduling
journal: Optimization Modeling for Supply Chain Applications
booktitle: Optimization Modeling for Supply Chain Applications
venue: Optimization Modeling for Supply Chain Applications

title: Metaheuristics Portfolio Optimization for Resource-Constrained Project Scheduling Problems with Milestones Payments
abstract:
Abstract
        Resource constrained project scheduling problem with milestone payments (RCPSPDCF-MP) is an intractable combinatorial problem. This has prompted researchers to propose a variety of metaheuristic approaches to address the problem. Hybridizing different metaheuristics to produce synergetic effect is a complex endeavor. In this study, we propose a distributed adaptive metaheuristics selection (DAMS) that leverages distributed computing nodes of modern computing architecture to hybridize heterogeneous metaheuristics, evaluated based on Chernoff-Hoeffding upper confidence bounds (UCB1) to solve RCPSPDCF-MP. Our DAMS framework selects a tailored set of metaheuristics for each problem instance (project) from five different metaheuristics customized to solve RCPSPDCF-MP. The parameters of each metaheuristic were tuned off-line using classical particle swarm optimization (PSO). Our proposed framework is examined using project sets from the test library, Project Scheduling Library (PSPLIB). Experiments show that the hybrid of metaheuristics generated by UCB1 based DAMS framework outperform existing methods from the literature.
publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC

title: Inventory Order Scheduling Optimization: Capacity-Constrained Fulfillment Window Allocation in Perishable Subscription E-Commerce
abstract:
Abstract
                We introduce the Capacity-Aware Perishable Fulfillment Scheduling (CAPFS) problem, a new class of combinatorial scheduling problem arising when subscription e-commerce orders must be assigned to weekday shipping slots under three simultaneous constraints: hard daily capacity limits at parallel fulfillment centers, customer-specific food-continuity deadlines derived from perishable on-hand inventory buffers, and order-type heterogeneity (trial versus regular) imposing asymmetric feasibility structures. We prove CAPFS is NP-hard by polynomial-time reduction from parallel machine scheduling with deadlines and weighted tardiness (Pm|r_j,d_j|ΣwjTj). We develop a MILP formulation with a multi-objective function covering capacity overload, aggregate stockout risk, and schedule lateness, alongside a polynomial O(n log n) Least-Slack Priority (LSP) heuristic with a provable structural safety property: early-order moves reduce both objectives simultaneously. Applied to 3,851 real meal-kit orders across two fulfillment centers in August 2020, the incumbent transit-time heuristic generates Tuesday overloads of 192–233% of capacity while Monday operates at 9–53% utilization — Jain Fairness Index 0.30–0.51 against a target of ≥0.90. The LSP heuristic achieves a 48.4% reduction in total capacity overload and 29.0%
publisher: Springer Science and Business Media LLC

title: Graph Attention Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization for Constrained Dynamic Scheduling in Industrial IoT-Edge Systems
Graph Attention Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization for Constrained Dynamic Scheduling in Industrial IoT-Edge Systems
abstract: The rapidly increasing Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) and Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) framework presents fundamental Operations Research (OR) challenges in managing dynamic, decentralized resource scheduling across heterogeneous industrial devices. Traditional centralized optimization methods fail under rapidly changing network conditions and complex device interactions, causing Service Level Agreement (SLA) violations for mission-critical services. This paper presents Graph Attention Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (GAT-MAPPO), a novel decentralized multi-agent reinforcement learning framework that integrates dynamic Graph Attention Networks (GAT) into MAPPO critic architecture for topology-aware constrained scheduling. We validate GAT-MAPPO through comprehensive industrial deployment at an operational automotive manufacturing facility spanning 12,000 m² with 78 heterogeneous devices including Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, KUKA robotic arms, and MiR AGVs connected via private 5G infrastructure. A ten-week deployment study comprising baseline characterization, shadow deployment, and full production operation demonstrates that GAT-MAPPO achieves 11.4 percentage point improvement in Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE), 83% reduction in network-related production stops, and 64% reduction in URLLC 99th percentile latency compared to the legacy priority-based scheduler. Simulation-to-reality validation shows strong correlation (R² = 0.94) between digital twin predictions and measured performance. The framework scales near-linearly to 100+ agents while maintaining sub-2ms inference latency, establishing GAT-MAPPO as a production-ready solution for next-generation smart manufacturing.

title: Graph Attention Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization for Constrained Dynamic Scheduling in Industrial IoT-Edge Systems
abstract: The rapidly increasing Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) and Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) framework presents fundamental Operations Research (OR) challenges in managing dynamic, decentralized resource scheduling across heterogeneous industrial devices. Traditional centralized optimization methods fail under rapidly changing network conditions and complex device interactions, causing Service Level Agreement (SLA) violations for mission-critical services. This paper presents Graph Attention Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (GAT-MAPPO), a novel decentralized multi-agent reinforcement learning framework that integrates dynamic Graph Attention Networks (GAT) into MAPPO critic architecture for topology-aware constrained scheduling. We validate GAT-MAPPO through comprehensive industrial deployment at an operational automotive manufacturing facility spanning 12,000 m² with 78 heterogeneous devices including Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, KUKA robotic arms, and MiR AGVs connected via private 5G infrastructure. A ten-week deployment study comprising baseline characterization, shadow deployment, and full production operation demonstrates that GAT-MAPPO achieves 11.4 percentage point improvement in Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE), 83% reduction in network-related production stops, and 64% reduction in URLLC 99th percentile latency compared to the legacy priority-based scheduler. Simulation-to-reality validation shows strong correlation (R² = 0.94) between digital twin predictions and measured performance. The framework scales near-linearly to 100+ agents while maintaining sub-2ms inference latency, establishing GAT-MAPPO as a production-ready solution for next-generation smart manufacturing.
